title: Visible calculated fields use type=text, not type=calculate
description: Computed values that must be visible to data entry use type=text + calculation + readonly=yes. type=calculate is hidden by OC from the data-entry view; use it ONLY for values consumed by other expressions (relevant, calculation, constraint downstream).
rationale: OC4 renders type=calculate as a non-displayed background calculation. If the value needs to appear in the data-entry UI (BMI from height/weight, derived eligibility verdict, repeat-instance label), the row must be type=text with calculation populated and readonly=yes. Forms that use type=calculate for display-bound values appear correct in the spec but show empty cells to the data-entry user.

flag.message: Field ${field.name} is type=calculate with a calculation. If this value should be visible to data entry, convert to type=text + readonly=yes. Use type=calculate ONLY for values consumed by other expressions (relevant/calculation/constraint), not for display.

effect.soft: When a calculate row's value is intended to be visible to the user — directly displayed, referenced in a label-bearing display field, or named in a way that suggests user-facing output — convert type=calculate to type=text and add readonly=yes. Reserve type=calculate for purely internal values consumed only by other expressions.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-5b during Phase B.1a. Distinguishing 'meant to be visible' from 'purely internal' requires AI judgment in many cases — peer rule to OC-5a but listed as structured because the type=calculate predicate is mechanical. The soft directive carries the visibility-intent guidance to Claude. Kind revised from `structured` to `hybrid` during B.1a translation because the 'value intended to be visible to data entry' predicate requires AI judgment; soft directive carries the visibility-intent guidance.
